arts.ttl: Potential Leeds United XI for Watford as Farke contemplates attacking change
arts.sum: They produced arguably their best performance of the season to date to beat promotion rivals Sheffield United last Friday so there's absolutely need for Daniel Farke to change a winning team, right? Potential United line-up vs Watford: Meslier; Bogle, Rodon, Struijk, Firpo; Rothwell, Tanaka; James, Gnonto, Ramazani; Joseph.

arts.ttl: Watford are too inconsistent at the basics says Cleverley
arts.sum: Tom Cleverley says his side will need to start doing the key things much better if they want to find some consistency.

arts.ttl: Leeds United's free agent rumours rubbished with training ground revelation
arts.sum: Space would have been an issue at Leeds United’s training ground had Daniel Farke welcomed in all of the free agents they have been linked with. The rumour mill went into overdrive after Farke publicly admitted he had hosted ‘special guests’ at Thorp Arch during the international break, having been asked about that free agent search.

arts.ttl: Leeds United squad revealed for Watford with three players ruled out amid Gnonto injury scare
arts.sum: Three points at home to Watford tonight will take Daniel Farke's team top of the Championship for the first time this season. They enter the Watford clash full of confidence and self belief, having arguably put in their best performance of the season against Sheffield United last time out.

arts.ttl: Tuesday's Championship predictions including Leeds vs. Watford
arts.sum: Sports Mole provides score predictions and previews for all of today's Championship fixtures, including Leeds vs. Watford.

arts.ttl: Is Leeds United vs Watford on TV? Kick-off time, live stream details and how to follow
arts.sum: Leeds United will be looking to stretch their unbeaten Championship run to six matches when they host eighth-placed Watford. How else can I follow Leeds United vs Watford?

arts.ttl: Tomorrow night will tell how Watford's players have reacted
arts.sum: A trip to Leeds on a Tuesday night is tough enough, but even more so after what happened at the weekend.

arts.ttl: Former Leeds United and Wolves defender makes transfer vow after sealing Scottish switch
arts.sum: The seasoned 35-year-old defender, with a career that spans from Queen’s Park to international appearances for Scotland, and clubs like Dundee United, Konyaspor, Wolves, Leeds United, Blackburn, and most recently Lech Poznan in Poland, is set to bring his wealth of experience to the Perth team now managed by Simo Valakari. He concluded with optimism about the team's prospects under Valakari's guidance: "There is definitely a lot of quality in the squad and I think when we start to work with the new manager and his philosophy, the only way is up really."

arts.ttl: 'I would not be happy' Farke pulls Leeds United curtain back on Bamford's status
arts.sum: Missed major parts of pre-season and when he came back he had more slight issues. A fully-fit Bamford would have had us in the PL last season.

arts.ttl: Leeds post TikTok poking fun at Sheff Utd’s O’Hare
arts.sum: Three Yorkshire derbies in the Championship this season for Leeds United have all yielded 2-0 wins, the latest coming against Sheffield United on Friday night. Leeds came into the weekend as the team to beat in the Championship, according to Blades boss Chris Wilder, and his praise of the club inherently put some pressure on the Whites to get the right result.
